content.text: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is the universal data exchange format for web applications. Every React app that communicates with an API reads and writes JSON. Understanding how JavaScript serializes to JSON (
) — and what each method silently drops or transforms — prevents a whole class of subtle data bugs.
JSON is a text format for structured data. It looks like JavaScript object and array literals but has strict rules:
Allowed value types: string, number, boolean, null, object, array
